bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no prior opponent offers or messages, I am targeting my maximum-utility opening bid. Since there is no evidence yet about opponent priorities, I will start by holding all issues at my most preferred value and wait for their counteroffer to reveal tradeoffs.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), and the opponent’s only offer was the minimum-value bid for me on every issue. To avoid simply repeating my opening bid while making only a small concession, I concede on low-weight issueA and hold firm on the more important issues, especially issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA offer, which is very poor for me and shows no concession so far; with no messages to consider, I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while avoiding repeating my last exact bid.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.06), while making a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid stalemate. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA and has not conceded, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues, especially issueF, while testing whether a concession on issueA helps.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.98 by conceding only on my lowest-weight issues while keeping issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD at valueC. The opponent has mostly held at all valueA but just improved issueF to valueB, so there is slight movement; since it is still very early, I should not make large concessions yet.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because the negotiation is very early at t=0.10, but I can concede on my lowest-weight issues first. The opponent has held firm on valueA for issues A-E while conceding issueF up to valueC, so I will keep my high-weight issues C-F at valueC and offer valueA on the two least important issues A and B.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid while making a limited concession because it is early in the negotiation at t=0.12. The opponent has mostly held at valueA but recently signaled movement on issueC and issueD, so I concede issueA and issueB fully and move issueC to valueB while holding my most important issueF and other valuable issues at valueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 while making a meaningful early concession at t=0.14 rather than dropping too far. The opponent has moved from all valueA to offering issueC=valueB, issueD=valueC, and issueE=valueB, and previously showed issueF=valueC, so I am accepting their movement on lower/moderate issues while holding firm on the most important issueF. This avoids repeating my prior bid and tests whether their recent concessions are stable.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making selective concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent has moved from all valueA toward valueB/valueC on some issues, especially issueB and issueE, so I can concede issueA/issueB and keep my highest-weight issueF at valueC.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making small concessions on lower-weight issues and one medium issue. The opponent has not consistently conceded, but they have signaled willingness for valueB on issueA/issueB and sometimes valueB/valueC on issueC/issueD/issueE/issueF, so I will keep my strongest positions on the high-weight issueF and important issueD/issueE while offering valueB on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 because we are still early at t=0.20, so I should not concede heavily on the most important issues, especially issueF, issueE, and issueD. The opponent has made some concessions, particularly showing willingness on issueC:valueB and issueD:valueC, but their offers remain inconsistent and low on my high-weight issues. I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issueB while keeping stronger values on the more important issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the low 0.8s, since time is only 0.22 and there is no need to concede to the opponent’s much lower offers yet. The opponent has recently shown willingness to give issueF=valueC and issueE=valueB, so I keep my highest-weight issueF firm while conceding on lower-weight issueA and partially on issueB/issueC/issueE to explore a plausible tradeoff.

I am targeting a still-strong utility by conceding mainly on low-weight issues A and B while holding the highest-weight issueF at valueC and keeping issueD at valueC. At t=0.24 there is some time pressure but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s low-value package; their offers show intermittent concessions on C, D, E, and F, with no natural-language messages to adjust that read.

At t=0.26 I am still targeting a relatively high utility around 0.78, since there is time left and the opponent has only partially conceded from all-valueA offers. Their latest bid signals willingness on issueC=valueB, issueD=valueB, and issueE=valueC, so I mirror those concessions while holding firm on issueF=valueC, my highest-weight issue.

At t=0.28 there is still time, so I am targeting a high but not maximal utility bid, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and some mid-weight issues the opponent has repeatedly touched. The opponent’s latest offer improves on issueB, issueD, and issueE but still asks for issueF=valueA, so I will hold firm on issueF as my most important issue while offering valueB on several others.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.83 because time is only at 0.30, so I should concede gradually but not drop to the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent has improved somewhat on issues like issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF across the history, but their latest bid is still much lower for me, so I will hold firm on high-importance issueF and issueE while conceding some lower/mid-weight ground.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility while making a meaningful concession on lower-weight issues A and B and partially aligning with the opponent’s latest bid on C and D. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight issueF and keep issueE at my best value rather than dropping too quickly. The opponent has been improving somewhat but is inconsistent, so I am offering a non-repeated compromise that matches several of their recent values while preserving my key utility.

I am targeting roughly 0.79 utility: still well above reservation, but with meaningful concessions now that we are in the early-mid phase. The opponent’s offers remain erratic and mostly low for me, though their latest bid included issueB: valueC, so I can safely take that while conceding on issueA and several mid-weight issues. With no natural-language messages, I rely only on the bid history and continue holding firm on my most important issue, issueF.

I am targeting a moderate utility concession around 0.71, still protecting the most important issueF at valueC while giving ground on lower-weight issueA and issueB and accepting valueB on several mid-weight issues. At t=0.36 there is time left, but the opponent has repeatedly offered valueA/valueB on many issues and most recently valueB on issueC, issueD, and issueF, so I am moving closer without dropping my key priority.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around the prior concession range while avoiding an exact repeat, keeping issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue. The opponent’s latest offer signals some willingness on issueA, issueD, and issueE, but they are still mostly demanding low values for me, so I concede mainly on lower-weight issues and hold firm on issueF.

At t=0.40 I am still targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.75 while keeping issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent has made some concessions but their offers to me remain relatively low, so I am conceding mainly on lower/mid-weight issues and aligning with values they have recently proposed where possible.

I am targeting a weighted utility around 0.71, making a moderate mid-game concession while still protecting issueF, which is my most important issue. The opponent has moved somewhat from all-valueA offers toward valueB on some issues, but their latest offer is still low for me, and there are no messages to change that assessment. I concede mainly on lower-weight issueB and keep the higher-value compromise level on issueC/D/E while holding issueF at valueC.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.706, keeping my highest-weight issueF at valueC while offering a tradeoff on issueC. At t=0.44 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s very low-utility pattern; their latest bid suggests they may tolerate valueB on issueA and issueB, so I use those while compromising on issueC.

I targeted a mid-game utility around 0.66 while preserving issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s latest bid already moved to valueB on issueB, issueD, and issueE, so I mirror most of that structure while conceding on lower/less promising issues and only holding firm on issueF.

At t=0.48 I am targeting a mid-high utility bid while still making visible concessions on lower-weight issues and on issueC, where the opponent frequently offers valueA. The opponent’s latest offer includes issueD=valueC, which is very valuable to me and therefore worth incorporating, while I continue to hold firm on the highest-weight issueF=valueC since they have previously offered it. No messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.75: still above the opponent’s recent offers to me, but with concessions on lower-weight issues and several valueB compromises. The opponent has not shown a steady concession pattern, though their latest bid includes issueF=valueC and issueB=valueB, so I preserve the high-weight issueF while building around those signals. There are no natural-language messages to weigh.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.665 while preserving issueF=valueC, my highest-weight issue, since the opponent has recently shown willingness to offer it. At t=0.52 there is some time pressure to concede, so I match their latest bid on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, while still asking for valueB on issueD and issueE rather than dropping fully to their last offer.

I targeted a mid-game utility around 0.66, keeping the high-weight issueF at valueC because the opponent has now repeatedly signaled willingness to offer it. Since time is only slightly past halfway, I am conceding on lower or less-protected issues by matching their latest issueA/issueB/issueC positions while holding issueD and issueE at valueB rather than dropping to valueA. There are no opponent messages, and their offers show gradual movement toward valueC on issueF but continued pressure for valueA elsewhere.

I am targeting about 0.66 utility: enough concession for the mid-game while still protecting my highest-weight issueF. The opponent’s latest offer improved on issueD and issueF compared with many earlier bids, but they still consistently ask for valueA on several issues; with no messages to consider, I use the bid history and make a near-counter to their last offer while upgrading issueF to my top value.

I am targeting roughly a 0.66 utility bid, which is a meaningful concession from my early high-utility offers while still protecting issueF, my highest-weight issue. At t=0.58 there is moderate time pressure, so I am mostly matching the opponent’s latest offer where possible and asking for only a small improvement on issueD from valueA to valueB. The opponent has recently conceded to valueC on issueF and valueB on issueE, so I preserve those signals while conceding low/mid-weight issues heavily.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.70 by holding firm on the highest-value/highest-weight issueF and accepting the opponent’s recent concession on issueC. At t=0.60 there is some time pressure, so I concede low-weight issueA/issueB and also issueE, while asking for a small improvement on issueD; the opponent’s offers show gradual movement toward valueC on issueF and issueC, with no natural-language messages to weigh.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.75 by holding firm on issueF, my highest-weight issue, while accepting several middle/low concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent has recently moved toward valueB on issueB/issueC/issueD/issueE but still pushed issueF down to valueA, so I am countering close to their latest structure while protecting issueF at valueC. Time is past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline, so I am conceding meaningfully without dropping to their low-utility offer.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 by holding firm on the most important issueF and keeping issueC/issueE at valueC, which the opponent has recently signaled some willingness to grant. At t=0.64 there is enough time to continue negotiating, but I should make meaningful concessions, so I concede issueA, issueB, and issueD to valueA. The opponent has no messages, and their offers show movement on issueC/issueE but persistent pressure against issueF, so I am not yet giving up issueF.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70: a meaningful concession from my previous 0.78 bid, but still above the opponent’s latest 0.62 utility-to-me offer. At t=0.66 there is some time pressure, so I concede on the medium-weight issueE while holding issueF and issueC at valueC, since the opponent has repeatedly shown willingness to offer valueC on those high-value issues. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting roughly the same utility as my last concessionary offer, around 0.70, because at t=0.68 there is meaningful time pressure but not enough to accept a very low bid. The opponent’s recent offers show willingness to give me valueC on issueD and issueE, while they push hard against issueF, so I concede issueF only to valueB while preserving gains on D/E.

I targeted a moderate utility offer around 0.665, since at t=0.70 we are in the late mid-game and need to move closer to bids the opponent has actually signaled. The opponent’s latest bid offers valueC on issueB and valueB on issueD/issueF, while earlier they have offered valueC on issueE, so I concede on issueA and issueC but keep issueE at valueC as a plausible tradeoff.

I am targeting a utility around 0.626, which is lower than my last counter but still safely above reservation and close to what the opponent has just signaled they would accept. At t=0.72, time pressure is meaningful, and the opponent’s recent valid offers have improved on my high-weight issues E and F, so matching their last bid is the strongest path to agreement. There are no opponent messages to discount.
